rationale: Neither candidate reproduces the one substantive ground-truth concern (yenuo26's nitpick that the new hasattr(reduce_scatter) checks don't actually verify reduce_scatter's behavior), so recall is low for both. X offers five grounded, file/line-anchored findings (missing pre-hook integration test, dp_metadata staleness assumption, undocumented blocking-collective invariant, undocumented cfg_parallel_size relaxation) that are plausible and diff-consistent, giving it real actionability even though most are minor/speculative. Y is essentially a rubber-stamp APPROVE with a single soft, non-actionable documentation nit and no test-coverage findings at all, despite the PR being almost entirely about test changes — a missed opportunity given the GT reviewer's actual concern was test thoroughness. X is more useful to a real reviewer despite neither hitting the ground truth precisely.
